# Secure Your Chiplet with Identity-Centric Security IP
Chiplet-based systems introduce new flexibility and new trust boundaries.
Crypto Quantique delivers hardware-anchored security IP purpose-built for heterogeneous chiplet integration, ensuring identity, attestation, and lifecycle management, without impacting performance or power budgets.
[Download Security IP Overview](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/)
![Wurth Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Wurth-Logo-Black.png) ![Siemens Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Siemens-Logo-Black-1.png) ![Renesas Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Renesas-Logo-Black.png) ![Microchip Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Microchip-Logo-2.png) ![IntelPro Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/IntelPro-Logo-Black.png) ![TCG Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/TCG-Logo-Black.png) ![Wurth Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Wurth-Logo-Black.png) ![Siemens Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Siemens-Logo-Black-1.png) ![Renesas Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Renesas-Logo-Black.png) ![Microchip Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Microchip-Logo-2.png) ![IntelPro Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/IntelPro-Logo-Black.png) ![TCG Logo](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/TCG-Logo-Black.png)
## Identity First: Trust Anchored in Every Die
Chiplet systems fragment traditional SoC trust models. Each die may come from a different vendor, process node, or supply chain stage.
We embed hardware-anchored identity directly into silicon using PUF-based Root of Trust IP. Every chiplet becomes uniquely identifiable and cryptographically bound to the system it joins.
This enables:
  * Unclonable per-die identity
  * Secure authentication between chiplets
  * Protection against substitution and overproduction
  * Clean trust boundaries across heterogeneous integration


Identity becomes foundational, not assumed.
## Security That Fits Constrained, High-Performance Architectures
Chiplet platforms often operate under strict PPA constraints, with accelerator-class performance requirements and distributed memory hierarchies.
Security cannot rely on heavy compute blocks or dedicated processors. It must integrate cleanly into:
  * AI accelerators and high-throughput fabrics
  * Memory-centric architectures
  * CPU-light control planes
  * Mixed-node or partitioned dies


Our IP is low-area, low-power, and designed to integrate without distorting architectural intent.
## Runtime Trust Across Distributed Systems
Chiplet-based systems extend beyond static boot security. Trust must persist across runtime, update, and operational phases. We enable:
  * Lightweight hardware-based attestation
  * Protection against fault injection and glitch attacks
  * Secure update and provisioning flows
  * Verification of distributed accelerator or memory domains


As systems scale across packages and deployments, trust remains anchored in silicon.
### Build Chiplet Systems That Are Verifiably Trustworthy
Our team can help you architect security around your chiplet system. Download our IP security product deck below, to learn more about our portfolio and product performance data.
[Download Security IP Overview](https://www.cryptoquantique.com/)

        <span class="inview" data-label="wysiwyg-col-1__5053eb20f9dbef9297fc9b946248e513"></span>
                <div class="v-mdu v-mdu-wysiwyg v-mdu-full-width py-3 py-lg-3 " id="wysiwyg-col-1__5053eb20f9dbef9297fc9b946248e513" data-aos="fade-up">
            <div class="container">
                <div class="row justify-content-start">
                    <div class="v-from-wysiwyg col-12 col-md-9 ">
                        <h2 class="wp-block-heading">Identity First: Trust Anchored in Every Die</h2>
<p data-start="662" data-end="796">Chiplet systems fragment traditional SoC trust models. Each die may come from a different vendor, process node, or supply chain stage.</p>
<p data-start="798" data-end="985">We embed hardware-anchored identity directly into silicon using PUF-based Root of Trust IP. Every chiplet becomes uniquely identifiable and cryptographically bound to the system it joins.</p>
<p data-start="987" data-end="1000">This enables:</p>
<ul data-start="1002" data-end="1191">
<li data-start="1002" data-end="1033">
<p data-start="1004" data-end="1033">Unclonable per-die identity</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1034" data-end="1076">
<p data-start="1036" data-end="1076">Secure authentication between chiplets</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1077" data-end="1131">
<p data-start="1079" data-end="1131">Protection against substitution and overproduction</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1132" data-end="1191">
<p data-start="1134" data-end="1191">Clean trust boundaries across heterogeneous integration</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1193" data-end="1237">Identity becomes foundational, not assumed.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
        
                </div>
            </div>

        <span class="inview" data-label="wysiwyg-col-1__33bd6004237f1e2328af2071f5255369"></span>
                <div class="v-mdu v-mdu-wysiwyg v-mdu-full-width py-3 py-lg-3 " id="wysiwyg-col-1__33bd6004237f1e2328af2071f5255369" data-aos="fade-up">
            <div class="container">
                <div class="row justify-content-start">
                    <div class="v-from-wysiwyg col-12 col-md-9 ">
                        <h2 class="wp-block-heading">Security That Fits Constrained, High-Performance Architectures</h2>
<p data-start="1314" data-end="1459">Chiplet platforms often operate under strict PPA constraints, with accelerator-class performance requirements and distributed memory hierarchies.</p>
<p data-start="1461" data-end="1562">Security cannot rely on heavy compute blocks or dedicated processors. It must integrate cleanly into:</p>
<ul data-start="1564" data-end="1708">
<li data-start="1564" data-end="1611">
<p data-start="1566" data-end="1611">AI accelerators and high-throughput fabrics</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1612" data-end="1644">
<p data-start="1614" data-end="1644">Memory-centric architectures</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1645" data-end="1673">
<p data-start="1647" data-end="1673">CPU-light control planes</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1674" data-end="1708">
<p data-start="1676" data-end="1708">Mixed-node or partitioned dies</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1710" data-end="1881">Our IP is low-area, low-power, and designed to integrate without distorting architectural intent.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

                <div class="v-mdu v-mdu-wysiwyg v-mdu-full-width py-1 py-lg-1 " id="wysiwyg-col-1__4113abb32316301ee811a481a6b1f7cf" data-aos="fade-up">
            <div class="container">
                <div class="row justify-content-start">
                    <div class="v-from-wysiwyg col-12 col-md-9 ">
                        <h2 class="wp-block-heading">Runtime Trust Across Distributed Systems</h2>
<p data-start="1936" data-end="2060">Chiplet-based systems extend beyond static boot security. Trust must persist across runtime, update, and operational phases. We enable:</p>
<ul data-start="2074" data-end="2277">
<li data-start="2074" data-end="2116">
<p data-start="2076" data-end="2116">Lightweight hardware-based attestation</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2117" data-end="2174">
<p data-start="2119" data-end="2174">Protection against fault injection and glitch attacks</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2175" data-end="2215">
<p data-start="2177" data-end="2215">Secure update and provisioning flows</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2216" data-end="2277">
<p data-start="2218" data-end="2277">Verification of distributed accelerator or memory domains</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2279" data-end="2363">As systems scale across packages and deployments, trust remains anchored in silicon.</p>
